---
title: "Microglia divergence in tau and amyloid AD models"
subtitle: "Highest-impact figures and results -- internal findings summary"
author: "Claude (autonomous agent)"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: !expr 'local({ source("R/report.R", local = TRUE); report_theme() })'
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
    fig_width: 8
    fig_height: 6
params:
  publication_mode: false
  cache_dir: "storage/cache"
  results_dir: "storage/results"
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center", dev = "png", dpi = 110, cache = FALSE
)
project_root <- normalizePath(".")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)
options(width = 110)

# Project-wide libraries + domain helpers (genotype_levels/_colours,
# contrast_definitions, proteomics_sample_meta, plot_kinase_activity_heatmap).
source("R/helpers.R")

# Summary-specific loaders the curated chunks assume:
#   rd() -> pre-extracted minimal plotting caches in storage/cache/
#   rt() -> exported result tables in storage/results/
rd <- function(f) readRDS(file.path(params$cache_dir, f))
rt <- function(f) read.delim(file.path(params$results_dir, f),
                             check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

# Overview {-}

This document curates the highest-impact figures and results from an integrated analysis of microglia across a 2x2 tau x amyloid mouse model, spanning single-nucleus and spatial transcriptomics, bulk proteomics and phosphoproteomics. The central finding is a *negative result with a constructive resolution*: tau-dependent amyloid remodelling of microglia does not appear as differentially expressed genes at the whole-microglia level in any modality, yet it is robustly present once the readout is resolved to substates, co-expression modules, neighbourhood abundance, kinase activity and intercellular signalling. The strongest single mechanism is **Gsk3b kinase activation at the tau x amyloid interaction** (padj 0.00152), a tau-dependent synergy.

This is an *internal synthesis*: phosphoproteomics involving the MAPT-KI background is shown, effect sizes and caveats are stated plainly, and findings are framed mechanistically rather than for publication. Every panel re-reads its data from disk (pre-extracted caches and exported result tables) and re-renders without recomputing the underlying analysis, so this report knits in roughly a minute and never touches the heavy Seurat or MultiNicheNet objects.

# Study design

The study crosses tau and amyloid pathology in a 2x2 factorial and reads each animal out across four molecular modalities. The divergence question -- does amyloid remodel microglia *differently* depending on the tau background -- is operationalised as a single interaction contrast.

## Cohort: four genotypes across four modalities

Four hippocampal genotypes span a 2x2 tau (wild-type humanised MAPT vs P301S) by amyloid (no-NLGF vs NLGF knock-in) design, profiled at 24 months. snRNAseq contributes 23,465 analysed microglia nuclei (drawn from a 286,287-nucleus all-cell-type atlas), GeoMx 91 microglia-enriched spatial AOIs, and bulk proteome and phosphoproteome 16 samples each (four biological replicates per genotype). Microglial nuclei roughly double under NLGF -- the first hint of amyloid-driven microglial expansion.

```{r design_cohort_table, results='asis'}
# Study-design cohort overview: 4 genotypes x 4 modalities, with the
# per-cell unit count and the 2x2 factor coding (tau, amyloid). All
# counts are read from authoritative light sources, never the heavy
# Seurat/MultiNicheNet caches:
#   * microglia nuclei per genotype  <- ccc_dam_sampling_balance_sample_dam.tsv
#       (the 23,465-nucleus analysed microglia subset; total column summed
#        over the 4 batches per genotype)
#   * GeoMx microglia AOIs per genotype <- storage/data/geomx.rds metadata
#       (23 MB object; sub-100 MB, may load directly)
#   * bulk proteome / phosphoproteome  <- proteomics_sample_key.csv (first
#       16 rows = the four 24-month genotypes, 4 replicates each)
# The full snRNAseq cohort spans 286,287 nuclei across all broad cell
# types; microglia are the analysed subset and are what the table reports.

design_cohort_geno <- genotype_levels

# --- snRNAseq: analysed microglia nuclei per genotype ---------------------
design_cohort_sd <- rt("ccc_dam_sampling_balance_sample_dam.tsv")
design_cohort_mg <- tapply(design_cohort_sd$total,
                           factor(design_cohort_sd$genotype, design_cohort_geno),
                           sum)
design_cohort_mg <- as.integer(design_cohort_mg[design_cohort_geno])

# --- GeoMx: microglia AOIs per genotype ----------------------------------
design_cohort_gx_obj <- readRDS("storage/data/geomx.rds")
design_cohort_gx <- as.integer(table(
  factor(design_cohort_gx_obj@meta.data$genotype, design_cohort_geno)
)[design_cohort_geno])
rm(design_cohort_gx_obj)

# --- bulk proteome / phosphoproteome: replicates per genotype ------------
design_cohort_key <- proteomics_sample_meta(
  "storage/data/proteomics_sample_key.csv", n_keep = 16
)
design_cohort_bulk <- as.integer(table(
  factor(design_cohort_key$genotype, design_cohort_geno)
)[design_cohort_geno])

# --- 2x2 factor coding ---------------------------------------------------
design_cohort_tau  <- ifelse(design_cohort_geno %in% c("P301S", "NLGF_P301S"),
                             "P301S", "wt-tau")
design_cohort_amy  <- ifelse(grepl("NLGF", design_cohort_geno), "NLGF", "no-NLGF")

# --- assemble (one row per genotype + a totals row) ----------------------
design_cohort_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  Genotype                       = design_cohort_geno,
  `Tau`                          = design_cohort_tau,
  `Amyloid`                      = design_cohort_amy,
  `snRNAseq (microglia nuclei)`  = design_cohort_mg,
  `GeoMx (microglia AOIs)`       = design_cohort_gx,
  `Proteome (samples)`           = design_cohort_bulk,
  `Phosphoproteome (samples)`    = design_cohort_bulk
)

design_cohort_total <- tibble::tibble(
  Genotype                       = "All four genotypes",
  `Tau`                          = "2x2",
  `Amyloid`                      = "2x2",
  `snRNAseq (microglia nuclei)`  = sum(design_cohort_mg),
  `GeoMx (microglia AOIs)`       = sum(design_cohort_gx),
  `Proteome (samples)`           = sum(design_cohort_bulk),
  `Phosphoproteome (samples)`    = sum(design_cohort_bulk)
)

design_cohort_out <- dplyr::bind_rows(design_cohort_tbl, design_cohort_total)

knitr::kable(
  design_cohort_out,
  align   = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
  caption = paste0(
    "Cohort overview: four hippocampal AD-model genotypes profiled across ",
    "four modalities. Genotypes form a 2x2 tau (wild-type humanised MAPT vs ",
    "P301S) by amyloid (no-NLGF vs NLGF knock-in) factorial. snRNAseq counts ",
    "are the analysed microglia subset (23,465 nuclei) drawn from a 286,287-",
    "nucleus all-cell-type atlas; GeoMx counts are microglia-enriched spatial ",
    "AOIs (91 total); bulk proteome and phosphoproteome share 16 samples ",
    "(4 biological replicates per genotype, 24-month timepoint)."
  )
)
```

## The 2x2 design and the interaction contrast

Five named contrasts sit on the design. The two amyloid (NLGF) effects and two tau (P301S) effects are the grid edges; the **interaction** -- (NLGF_P301S - P301S) - (NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI) -- is the differential-of-differentials that isolates tau-dependent amyloid remodelling. It is the project's core readout: the single strongest mechanism (Gsk3b kinase activation, padj 0.00152) concentrates here, even though the whole-microglia transcriptional interaction is essentially null.

```{r design_factorial_contrasts, fig.width=8.5, fig.height=6}
# 2x2 factorial layout of the four genotypes plus the five named contrasts
# that drive the divergence narrative. Pure design schematic: built only
# from the project constants genotype_levels / genotype_colours and the
# contrast_definitions list (loaded by R/helpers.R); no data cache touched.
# The interaction contrast is the project's core readout and is drawn as
# the differential-of-differentials of the two amyloid (NLGF) effects.

# --- place each genotype on the 2x2 grid ---------------------------------
# x-axis = amyloid (NLGF) status, y-axis = tau (P301S) status.
design_fc_pos <- tibble::tibble(
  genotype = genotype_levels,
  amyloid  = ifelse(grepl("NLGF", genotype_levels), 1L, 0L),
  tau      = ifelse(genotype_levels %in% c("P301S", "NLGF_P301S"), 1L, 0L)
)
design_fc_pos$x <- design_fc_pos$amyloid
design_fc_pos$y <- design_fc_pos$tau

design_fc_lookup <- function(g) design_fc_pos[match(g, design_fc_pos$genotype), c("x", "y")]

# --- the five canonical contrasts (numerator - denominator) --------------
# Four pairwise contrasts come straight from contrast_definitions; the
# fifth (interaction) is the difference of the two NLGF effects.
design_fc_named <- contrast_definitions[c("nlgf_in_maptki", "nlgf_in_p301s",
                                          "tau_alone", "tau_in_nlgf")]
design_fc_arrows <- purrr::imap_dfr(design_fc_named, function(pair, nm) {
  num <- design_fc_lookup(pair[1]); den <- design_fc_lookup(pair[2])
  tibble::tibble(contrast = nm,
                 x = den$x, y = den$y, xend = num$x, yend = num$y)
})
# Nudge the two horizontal NLGF arrows vertically apart so both labels read.
design_fc_arrows$kind <- ifelse(
  design_fc_arrows$contrast %in% c("nlgf_in_maptki", "nlgf_in_p301s"),
  "amyloid effect", "tau effect"
)
design_fc_lab <- c(
  nlgf_in_maptki = "nlgf_in_maptki\n(NLGF effect, wt-tau)",
  nlgf_in_p301s  = "nlgf_in_p301s\n(NLGF effect, P301S)",
  tau_alone      = "tau_alone\n(P301S effect, no-NLGF)",
  tau_in_nlgf    = "tau_in_nlgf\n(P301S effect, NLGF)"
)
design_fc_arrows$label <- design_fc_lab[design_fc_arrows$contrast]
design_fc_arrows$mx <- (design_fc_arrows$x + design_fc_arrows$xend) / 2
design_fc_arrows$my <- (design_fc_arrows$y + design_fc_arrows$yend) / 2

# --- build the plot ------------------------------------------------------
design_fc_pos$lab <- factor(design_fc_pos$genotype, levels = genotype_levels)

design_fc_p <- ggplot() +
  # contrast arrows between adjacent cells: amyloid effects darker, tau
  # effects lighter, both fixed greys (no mapped colour scale, so the
  # genotype fill below is the figure's only legendable scale).
  geom_segment(
    data = design_fc_arrows,
    aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
    colour = ifelse(design_fc_arrows$kind == "amyloid effect",
                    "#6f6f6f", "#b0b0b0"),
    arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = design_fc_arrows,
    aes(x = mx, y = my, label = label),
    colour = ifelse(design_fc_arrows$kind == "amyloid effect",
                    "#4d4d4d", "#8a8a8a"),
    size = 2.5, label.size = 0, fill = "white",
    label.padding = grid::unit(0.1, "lines"), lineheight = 0.85
  ) +
  # genotype cells, coloured by the canonical palette
  geom_point(data = design_fc_pos, aes(x, y, fill = lab),
             shape = 21, size = 18, colour = "grey20", stroke = 0.6) +
  geom_text(data = design_fc_pos, aes(x, y, label = genotype),
            size = 2.7, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genotype_colours, guide = "none") +
  # interaction annotation across the top
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 1.46,
           label = "INTERACTION (core readout):", fontface = "bold", size = 3.1) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 1.34,
           label = "(NLGF_P301S - P301S) - (NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI)",
           size = 3.0, fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.5, xend = 0.5, y = 1.27, yend = 1.06,
           arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
           colour = "#B0344D", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 1.15,
           label = "= divergence of the\nNLGF effect across tau backgrounds",
           size = 2.6, lineheight = 0.85, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
           colour = "#B0344D") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 1), labels = c("no-NLGF", "NLGF (amyloid)"),
                     limits = c(-0.45, 1.45)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 1), labels = c("wt-tau", "P301S (tau)"),
                     limits = c(-0.35, 1.55)) +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  labs(
    x = "Amyloid axis", y = "Tau axis",
    title = "2x2 factorial design and the five named contrasts",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Four genotypes cross tau (P301S) with amyloid (NLGF). The interaction ",
      "contrast isolates how\namyloid reshapes microglia differently on a ",
      "P301S vs a wild-type-tau background."
    ),
    caption = paste0(
      "Core readout where the divergence signal lives (e.g. Gsk3b kinase ",
      "activation at the interaction, padj 0.00152); the whole-microglia ",
      "transcriptional interaction is near-null."
    )
  )
design_fc_p
```

# The microglial atlas

Microglia resolve into four transcriptional substates, and amyloid reshapes their proportions far more than tau does.

## DAM doubles under amyloid

Across 26,104 microglial nuclei (pre-QC-pruning; substates by AddModuleScore argmax), the disease-associated (DAM) fraction roughly doubles under amyloid on both tau backgrounds -- MAPTKI 19.8% to 40.1%, P301S 19.3% to 41.3% -- at the expense of the homeostatic compartment (~65% to ~44%). Tau alone barely shifts composition. This additive amyloid expansion is the compositional backdrop against which every downstream divergence readout is interpreted.

```{r mg_substate_composition, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.8}
# Microglia substate composition by genotype. Reproduces the substate
# stacked-bar from rmd/02a_snrnaseq_qc.Rmd (chunk snrnaseq-substate),
# loading the pre-extracted per-cell table instead of the 730M Seurat.
# Cache is PRE-QC-pruning (26,104 microglial nuclei; argmax of
# AddModuleScore over homeostatic/DAM/IFN/proliferative signatures).
mg_comp_df <- rd("summary_mg_substate_umap.rds")

# Composition within each genotype (matches prop.table(margin = 2) in source).
mg_comp_tab  <- table(mg_comp_df$state, mg_comp_df$genotype)
mg_comp_prop <- prop.table(mg_comp_tab, margin = 2)
mg_comp_long <- as.data.frame(mg_comp_prop)
names(mg_comp_long) <- c("state", "genotype", "prop")
mg_comp_long$genotype <- factor(mg_comp_long$genotype, levels = genotype_levels)

# Explicit substate palette (stable; Set2 hues as used in the source chunk).
mg_comp_pal <- c(
  homeostatic   = "#1B9E77",
  DAM           = "#B0344D",
  IFN           = "#7570B3",
  proliferative = "#E08754"
)

# DAM labels: amyloid (NLGF) roughly doubles the DAM fraction under both
# tau backgrounds (MAPTKI 19.8% -> 40.1%; P301S 19.3% -> 41.3%).
mg_comp_dam <- mg_comp_long[mg_comp_long$state == "DAM", ]
mg_comp_dam$lab <- sprintf("%.0f%%", mg_comp_dam$prop * 100)

ggplot(mg_comp_long, aes(genotype, prop, fill = state)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    data = mg_comp_dam,
    aes(label = lab), position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5),
    colour = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, inherit.aes = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mg_comp_pal) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Fraction of microglial nuclei", fill = "Substate",
    title = "Microglia substate composition by genotype",
    subtitle = "DAM fraction (labelled) roughly doubles with amyloid (NLGF) under both tau backgrounds",
    caption = "snRNAseq, 26,104 microglial nuclei (pre-QC-pruning); substates by AddModuleScore argmax"
  )
```

# The divergence is null at the whole-microglia level

The natural expectation -- that tau-dependent amyloid remodelling shows up as differentially expressed genes -- fails. At the whole-microglia level the interaction contrast is null, and the null is robust to method, modality and analytic shrinkage.

## snRNAseq interaction volcano (NEBULA)

Single-cell NEBULA (negative-binomial GLMM, 23,465 cells x 11,411 genes) finds no gene clearing FDR < 0.05 at the interaction; the leading hit Plac9 sits at FDR 0.063. The cloud is centred on the origin -- there is no whole-microglia interaction programme to speak of.

```{r sn_nebula_interaction_volcano, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=5.2}
sn_neb_vol_tbl <- rd("summary_sn_nebula_interaction_volcano.rds")
sn_neb_vol_tbl$sig <- sn_neb_vol_tbl$adj.P.Val < 0.10 & abs(sn_neb_vol_tbl$logFC) > 0.5
# A single predicted-gene outlier (Gm44993, logFC ~ -25, P ~ 0.99) otherwise
# stretches the x-axis and crushes the near-null cloud; clip the view to focus
# on the informative range. All 15 labels lie within this window; coord_cartesian
# clips display only (no points dropped from the data or stats).
sn_neb_vol_xlim <- c(-5, 5)
sn_neb_vol_lab <- sn_neb_vol_tbl |>
  dplyr::arrange(P.Value) |>
  head(15)
sn_neb_vol_p <- ggplot(sn_neb_vol_tbl, aes(logFC, -log10(P.Value))) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = sig), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey75", `TRUE` = "#b40426"),
                      guide = "none") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = sn_neb_vol_lab, aes(label = symbol),
                           size = 3, max.overlaps = 50, min.segment.length = 0) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = sn_neb_vol_xlim) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "snRNAseq interaction contrast (NEBULA single-cell NB GLMM)",
    subtitle = "(NLGF_P301S - P301S) - (NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI); 23,465 cells x 11,411 genes",
    x = expression(log[2]~fold-change), y = expression(-log[10]~italic(P)),
    caption = paste0(
      "Leading hit Plac9 at FDR 0.063; no gene clears FDR < 0.05. ",
      "One off-scale predicted-gene outlier (Gm44993, P ~ 0.99) hidden by x-axis clipping."
    )
  )
sn_neb_vol_p
```

## Six-method concordance of the interaction

The null is not a NEBULA artefact. Six DE methods spanning three shrinkage families agree: pairwise Spearman concordance of interaction log2FC (and of -log10 P) is uniformly high *among methods*, yet they all rank the same near-zero effects -- method choice does not rescue a signal.

```{r sn_triangulation_concordance, fig.width=10, fig.height=5.2}
sn_tri_tops <- rd("summary_sn_triangulation_interaction.rds")
sn_tri_int <- purrr::imap(sn_tri_tops, function(t, mname) {
  t |>
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(logFC), is.finite(P.Value), P.Value > 0) |>
    dplyr::transmute(gene,
                     "{mname}_logFC"   := logFC,
                     "{mname}_negLogP" := -log10(P.Value))
})
sn_tri_joined <- Reduce(function(a, b) dplyr::inner_join(a, b, by = "gene"),
                        sn_tri_int)
sn_tri_lfc <- as.matrix(sn_tri_joined[, grep("_logFC$",   names(sn_tri_joined))])
sn_tri_plp <- as.matrix(sn_tri_joined[, grep("_negLogP$", names(sn_tri_joined))])
colnames(sn_tri_lfc) <- sub("_logFC$",   "", colnames(sn_tri_lfc))
colnames(sn_tri_plp) <- sub("_negLogP$", "", colnames(sn_tri_plp))
sn_tri_rho_l <- cor(sn_tri_lfc, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
sn_tri_rho_p <- cor(sn_tri_plp, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

sn_tri_corp <- function(mat, title) {
  df <- as.data.frame.table(mat, responseName = "rho")
  ggplot(df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = rho)) +
    geom_tile(colour = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", rho)), size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#3a4cc0", mid = "white", high = "#b40426",
                         midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
          panel.grid = element_blank()) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = title, fill = "Spearman\nrho")
}
sn_tri_p <- (sn_tri_corp(sn_tri_rho_l, "Interaction log2FC concordance") |
             sn_tri_corp(sn_tri_rho_p, "Interaction -log10(P) concordance")) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "snRNAseq interaction contrast: six-method cross-concordance",
    subtitle = sprintf("%d genes common to all six methods; two scales x three shrinkage families",
                       nrow(sn_tri_joined)))
sn_tri_p
```

## Spatial validates the main effects, not the interaction

Spatial transcriptomics corroborates. GeoMx reproduces the snRNAseq amyloid main effects (Spearman rho 0.117 in MAPTKI, 0.208 in P301S) but the interaction contrast is null cross-modally (rho -0.048, flat-to-negative slope). The spatial modality is not globally noisy -- the main effects validate -- so the interaction's absence is real, not a sensitivity floor.

```{r gx_sn_interaction_concordance, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
gx_sc_tbl <- rd("summary_gx_sn_interaction_concordance.rds")
# Source: rmd/07_integration.Rmd `concordance_plot()` over the harmonised
# integration log2FC table (snRNAseq column = single-cell NEBULA fit).
# We replicate that scatter for the two NLGF main effects and the
# tau x amyloid interaction, to ask whether the spatial modality
# reproduces the single-cell directionality.
gx_sc_levels <- c(
  nlgf_in_maptki = "NLGF effect in MAPTKI (amyloid, tau-WT background)",
  nlgf_in_p301s  = "NLGF effect in P301S (amyloid, mutant-tau background)",
  interaction    = "Interaction (tau-dependent NLGF response)"
)
gx_sc_one <- function(cn) {
  sub <- gx_sc_tbl |> dplyr::filter(contrast == cn)
  rho_s <- suppressWarnings(cor(sub$snrnaseq, sub$geomx, method = "spearman"))
  rho_p <- suppressWarnings(cor(sub$snrnaseq, sub$geomx, method = "pearson"))
  sub$score <- abs(sub$snrnaseq) + abs(sub$geomx)
  top <- sub |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(score)) |> head(12)
  ggplot(sub, aes(snrnaseq, geomx)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "#1f77b4") +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = top, aes(label = symbol),
                             size = 3, max.overlaps = 50) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      x = "snRNAseq log2FC (NEBULA single-cell)", y = "GeoMx spatial log2FC",
      title = gx_sc_levels[[cn]],
      subtitle = sprintf("Spearman rho = %.3f, Pearson r = %.3f, n = %d",
                         rho_s, rho_p, nrow(sub))
    )
}
gx_sc_p <- (gx_sc_one("nlgf_in_maptki") | gx_sc_one("nlgf_in_p301s")) /
  (gx_sc_one("interaction") + patchwork::plot_spacer()) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Spatial microglia validate the NLGF main effects but not the tau x amyloid interaction",
    theme = ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"))
  )
gx_sc_p
```

## Cross-modality concordance matrix

Pooling all four modalities, the only appreciable cross-modality agreement sits on the proteome-phospho axis (proteome vs phospho rho 0.21-0.35; phospho vs corrected-phospho 0.47-0.53). Transcript-versus-protein concordance is near-null throughout, including at the interaction. This is the empirical case for abandoning a whole-microglia transcriptional readout and pivoting to mechanism-resolved layers.

```{r xmod_concordance_heatmap, fig.width=11, fig.height=5}
xmod_ch_tbl <- rt("modality_concordance.tsv")
# Source: rmd/07_integration.Rmd `concordance-summary` chunk. That chunk
# computes Spearman/Pearson concordance of harmonised gene-level log2FC for
# every modality pair within each contrast (snRNAseq column = single-cell
# NEBULA fit; phospho aggregated to gene by max |log2FC| across sites). We
# render the Spearman matrix as a ComplexHeatmap, split by the three framing
# contrasts, to show WHERE cross-modality agreement does and does not exist.
xmod_ch_mods   <- c("snrnaseq", "geomx", "proteomics", "phospho", "phospho_corr")
xmod_ch_labels <- c(snrnaseq = "snRNAseq", geomx = "GeoMx",
                    proteomics = "Proteome", phospho = "Phospho",
                    phospho_corr = "Phospho (corr.)")
xmod_ch_contr  <- c(nlgf_in_maptki = "NLGF in MAPTKI",
                    nlgf_in_p301s  = "NLGF in P301S",
                    interaction    = "Interaction")

# Build a symmetric Spearman matrix per contrast (diagonal = 1).
xmod_ch_mat_for <- function(cn) {
  m <- matrix(NA_real_, length(xmod_ch_mods), length(xmod_ch_mods),
              dimnames = list(xmod_ch_mods, xmod_ch_mods))
  diag(m) <- 1
  sub <- xmod_ch_tbl[xmod_ch_tbl$contrast == cn, ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub))) {
    m[sub$x[i], sub$y[i]] <- sub$spearman[i]
    m[sub$y[i], sub$x[i]] <- sub$spearman[i]
  }
  dimnames(m) <- list(xmod_ch_labels[xmod_ch_mods], xmod_ch_labels[xmod_ch_mods])
  m
}

xmod_ch_col <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-0.5, 0, 0.5),
                                    c("#3b4cc0", "white", "#b40426"))

xmod_ch_one <- function(cn, is_first) {
  m <- xmod_ch_mat_for(cn)
  ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(
    m,
    name = paste0("rho_", cn),
    col = xmod_ch_col,
    cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
    column_title = xmod_ch_contr[[cn]],
    column_title_gp = grid::gpar(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 11),
    row_names_side = "left", show_row_names = is_first,
    column_names_rot = 45, column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),
    row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 9),
    rect_gp = grid::gpar(col = "grey85", lwd = 0.5),
    na_col = "grey92",
    show_heatmap_legend = is_first,
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
      title = "Spearman\nrho", at = c(-0.5, 0, 0.5),
      legend_height = grid::unit(3, "cm")
    ),
    cell_fun = function(jj, ii, x, y, w, h, fill) {
      v <- m[ii, jj]
      if (!is.na(v) && ii != jj) {
        grid::grid.text(sprintf("%.2f", v), x, y,
                        gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 7.5,
                                        col = if (abs(v) > 0.32) "white" else "grey20"))
      }
    },
    width = grid::unit(4.4, "cm"), height = grid::unit(4.4, "cm")
  )
}

xmod_ch_ht <- xmod_ch_one("nlgf_in_maptki", TRUE) +
  xmod_ch_one("nlgf_in_p301s", FALSE) +
  xmod_ch_one("interaction", FALSE)

ComplexHeatmap::draw(
  xmod_ch_ht,
  column_title = paste0(
    "Cross-modality log2FC concordance is carried by the proteome-phospho axis, ",
    "not by transcript-protein agreement"),
  column_title_gp = grid::gpar(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 12),
  ht_gap = grid::unit(6, "mm")
)
```

# Where the divergence actually lives

The divergence has not vanished -- it has moved off the whole-microglia gene axis. Four orthogonal readouts localise it: to specific substates, to coordinated pathways, to an unsupervised co-expression module, and to neighbourhood abundance.

## IFN microglia carry a tau-context-dependent response

Within IFN microglia the amyloid response is sharply tau-context-dependent: 446 genes respond to NLGF only on the P301S background versus 56 only on MAPTKI (82 shared), so the P301S-specific cloud lifts off the identity line. This per-substate asymmetry is the mathematical substrate of the otherwise-null whole-microglia interaction, and Pros1 -- the surviving TAM-kinase route from the synaptic axis -- is among the top P301S-specific responders.

```{r sn_per_state_ifn_asymmetry, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=6.5}
# Per-substate localisation of the divergence signal (gene level).
# The whole-microglia interaction contrast is null gene-by-gene, and it
# stays null WITHIN every substate (0 genes at FDR<0.05 in all 4 states;
# see nebula_per_state_summary.tsv). The divergence is instead localised
# as an ASYMMETRIC NLGF response: IFN microglia mount a far broader
# transcriptional response to amyloid in the P301S tau background
# (nlgf_in_p301s) than in MAPTKI (nlgf_in_maptki). This scatter projects
# the two within-IFN NLGF contrasts against each other; points off the
# dashed identity line are tau-context-dependent (the mathematical
# substrate of the whole-microglia interaction).
sn_ifn_asym <- rt("ifn_nlgf_asymmetry.tsv")
sn_ifn_asym$category <- factor(
  sn_ifn_asym$category,
  levels = c("Shared", "P301S-specific", "MAPTKI-specific", "ns")
)

# Label the strongest drivers per category, mirroring the source section
# (top by P, plus a few shared anchors). |logFC|<=10 guards against
# NEBULA's extreme natural-log estimates for near-zero-count genes.
sn_ifn_lab_n <- 12L
sn_ifn_label <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  sn_ifn_asym |>
    dplyr::filter(category == "P301S-specific",
                  abs(logFC_p301s) <= 10, abs(logFC_maptki) <= 10) |>
    dplyr::arrange(P_p301s) |> head(sn_ifn_lab_n),
  sn_ifn_asym |>
    dplyr::filter(category == "MAPTKI-specific",
                  abs(logFC_p301s) <= 10, abs(logFC_maptki) <= 10) |>
    dplyr::arrange(P_maptki) |> head(sn_ifn_lab_n),
  sn_ifn_asym |>
    dplyr::filter(category == "Shared",
                  abs(logFC_p301s) <= 10, abs(logFC_maptki) <= 10) |>
    dplyr::arrange(pmin(P_p301s, P_maptki)) |> head(6L)
)

# Per-category counts (FDR<0.05) for the subtitle.
sn_ifn_counts <- as.list(table(sn_ifn_asym$category))
sn_ifn_sub <- sprintf(
  paste0("IFN substate, FDR<0.05: %d P301S-specific vs %d MAPTKI-specific ",
         "(%d shared). Points off the identity line = tau-context-dependent ",
         "amyloid response."),
  sn_ifn_counts[["P301S-specific"]],
  sn_ifn_counts[["MAPTKI-specific"]],
  sn_ifn_counts[["Shared"]]
)

sn_ifn_cols <- c(
  Shared            = "#7e3a86",
  `P301S-specific`  = "#b40426",
  `MAPTKI-specific` = "#3b4cc0",
  ns                = "grey75"
)

sn_ifn_asym_p <- ggplot(
  sn_ifn_asym,
  aes(logFC_maptki, logFC_p301s, colour = category)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey85") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey85") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              colour = "grey60") +
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(sn_ifn_asym, category == "ns"),
             alpha = 0.18, size = 0.6) +
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(sn_ifn_asym, category != "ns"),
             alpha = 0.85, size = 1.2) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = sn_ifn_label, aes(label = symbol),
    size = 3, colour = "black", max.overlaps = 40,
    min.segment.length = 0, segment.colour = "grey60"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = sn_ifn_cols, name = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6, 6), ylim = c(-6, 6)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    x = expression(log[2]~FC~", NLGF effect in MAPTKI tau (IFN)"),
    y = expression(log[2]~FC~", NLGF effect in P301S tau (IFN)"),
    title = "IFN microglia carry the divergence: amyloid response is tau-context-dependent",
    subtitle = sn_ifn_sub,
    caption = paste0(
      "Per-substate NEBULA NB-GLMM (5% prevalence filter). Display clipped to ",
      "|logFC|<=6; labels are top hits by P within each category. ",
      "Pros1 (TAM-kinase route) is a top P301S-specific responder."
    )
  )
sn_ifn_asym_p
```

## Per-substate differential-expression summary

Quantifying the localisation: the interaction is null in *every* substate (0 genes at FDR < 0.05), confirming the whole-microglia null is not a substate-averaging artefact. The divergence reads off the asymmetry between the two NLGF arms -- IFN is P301S-leaning (528 vs 138 significant genes) whereas proliferative inverts to MAPTKI-leaning (139 vs 500) -- and genes reappear under the amyloid-conditioned tau effect (tau_in_nlgf: IFN 101, DAM 25).

```{r sn_per_state_de_summary, results='asis'}
# Per-substate NEBULA DE summary: how many genes move at FDR<0.05 for
# each of the five 2x2 contrasts, within each microglia substate. This
# is the localisation backbone for the divergence story:
#   * interaction == 0 in EVERY substate (the whole-microglia gene-level
#     null is reproduced per state, not hidden by substate averaging);
#   * the divergence surfaces as ASYMMETRY between the two NLGF arms -
#     IFN: 528 (P301S) vs 138 (MAPTKI); proliferative inverts to
#     500 (MAPTKI) vs 139 (P301S);
#   * tau_in_nlgf (the amyloid-conditioned tau effect) is where genes do
#     reappear (IFN 101, DAM 25).
sn_pss <- rt("nebula_per_state_summary.tsv")

sn_pss_contrasts <- c("nlgf_in_maptki", "nlgf_in_p301s",
                      "interaction", "tau_alone", "tau_in_nlgf")
sn_pss_contrast_lab <- c(
  nlgf_in_maptki = "NLGF in MAPTKI",
  nlgf_in_p301s  = "NLGF in P301S",
  interaction    = "Interaction",
  tau_alone      = "Tau alone",
  tau_in_nlgf    = "Tau in NLGF"
)
sn_pss_state_order <- c("homeostatic", "DAM", "IFN", "proliferative")

sn_pss <- sn_pss |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    State    = factor(state, levels = sn_pss_state_order),
    Contrast = factor(sn_pss_contrast_lab[contrast],
                      levels = unname(sn_pss_contrast_lab)),
    `n sig (FDR<0.05)` = n_sig,
    `up / down`        = paste0(n_up, " / ", n_down),
    `min FDR`          = signif(min_padj, 3)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(State, Contrast) |>
  dplyr::select(State, Contrast, `n genes` = n_genes,
                `n sig (FDR<0.05)`, `up / down`, `min FDR`)

knitr::kable(
  sn_pss,
  caption = paste0(
    "Per-substate NEBULA differential-expression summary across the five ",
    "2x2 factorial contrasts (genes at FDR<0.05; gene universe filtered to ",
    ">=5% prevalence within each substate). The interaction contrast is ",
    "null in every substate, so the divergence is read off the asymmetry ",
    "between the two NLGF arms - strongest and oppositely signed in IFN ",
    "(528 vs 138, P301S-leaning) versus proliferative (139 vs 500, ",
    "MAPTKI-leaning)."
  )
)
```

## Pathway suppression localises to DAM and homeostatic

Where individual genes are silent, coordinated pathways are not. GSEA on the per-state NEBULA t-statistics recovers 43 GO BP terms at padj < 0.1 in at least one substate, ~90% negatively signed: oxidative phosphorylation, cellular respiration, cytoplasmic translation and ribosome biogenesis are suppressed at the interaction, predominantly in DAM and homeostatic microglia (e.g. DAM ribonucleoprotein-complex biogenesis NES -1.73, padj 7.1e-4).

```{r sn_per_state_pathway_breadth, fig.width=8.5, fig.height=8.5}
# Per-substate pathway breadth at the interaction contrast.
# Gene-by-gene the interaction is null in every substate, yet GSEA on the
# per-state NEBULA t-statistics recovers a COHERENT pathway-level
# divergence - concentrated in DAM and homeostatic microglia, and almost
# uniformly NEGATIVE (oxidative phosphorylation / cellular respiration /
# cytoplasmic translation / ribosome biogenesis suppressed at the
# tau x amyloid interaction). This is the OXPHOS-leaning signal flagged
# in the whole-microglia analysis, here localised by substate.
sn_pb_fg <- rt("fgsea_per_state.tsv")
sn_pb_fg$padj <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(sn_pb_fg$padj))
sn_pb_fg$NES  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(sn_pb_fg$NES))

sn_pb_state_order <- c("homeostatic", "DAM", "IFN", "proliferative")

# GO BP, interaction contrast only. Keep pathways significant (padj<0.1)
# in at least one substate, then cap to the most divergent terms by
# max|NES| across states so labels stay legible.
sn_pb_int <- sn_pb_fg |>
  dplyr::filter(collection == "gobp", contrast == "interaction") |>
  dplyr::select(pathway, state, NES, padj) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols = pathway, names_from = state,
    values_from = c(NES, padj), names_glue = "{.value}_{state}"
  )

sn_pb_nes_cols  <- grep("^NES_",  names(sn_pb_int), value = TRUE)
sn_pb_padj_cols <- grep("^padj_", names(sn_pb_int), value = TRUE)
sn_pb_sig <- apply(as.matrix(sn_pb_int[, sn_pb_padj_cols]) < 0.1, 1,
                   function(r) any(r, na.rm = TRUE))
sn_pb_int <- sn_pb_int[sn_pb_sig, , drop = FALSE]

sn_pb_mat <- as.matrix(sn_pb_int[, sn_pb_nes_cols])
rownames(sn_pb_mat) <- gsub("_", " ", sub("^GOBP_", "", sn_pb_int$pathway))
colnames(sn_pb_mat) <- sub("^NES_", "", colnames(sn_pb_mat))
sn_pb_mat[!is.finite(sn_pb_mat)] <- 0
# Order columns into the canonical substate order.
sn_pb_mat <- sn_pb_mat[, intersect(sn_pb_state_order, colnames(sn_pb_mat)),
                       drop = FALSE]
if (nrow(sn_pb_mat) > 35) {
  sn_pb_keep <- order(apply(abs(sn_pb_mat), 1, max, na.rm = TRUE),
                      decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(35)]
  sn_pb_mat <- sn_pb_mat[sn_pb_keep, , drop = FALSE]
}
# Sort rows by DAM NES (the lead carrier) for a readable gradient.
sn_pb_sortcol <- if ("DAM" %in% colnames(sn_pb_mat)) "DAM" else colnames(sn_pb_mat)[1]
sn_pb_mat <- sn_pb_mat[order(sn_pb_mat[, sn_pb_sortcol]), , drop = FALSE]

# Significance overlay: star where per-state padj<0.1.
sn_pb_padj_mat <- as.matrix(sn_pb_int[, sn_pb_padj_cols])
rownames(sn_pb_padj_mat) <- rownames(as.matrix(sn_pb_int[, sn_pb_nes_cols]))
colnames(sn_pb_padj_mat) <- sub("^padj_", "", colnames(sn_pb_padj_mat))
rownames(sn_pb_padj_mat) <- gsub("_", " ", sub("^GOBP_", "", sn_pb_int$pathway))
sn_pb_padj_mat <- sn_pb_padj_mat[rownames(sn_pb_mat), colnames(sn_pb_mat),
                                 drop = FALSE]

sn_pb_col <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-2.5, 0, 2.5),
                                  c("#3a4cc0", "white", "#b40426"))

ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(
  sn_pb_mat,
  name = "NES",
  col = sn_pb_col,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  row_title = "GO BP pathway",
  column_title = sprintf(
    paste0("Per-substate GO BP enrichment at the tau x amyloid interaction ",
           "(top %d by max|NES|; * = padj<0.1)"),
    nrow(sn_pb_mat)
  ),
  row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 7),
  column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 10),
  column_names_rot = 0,
  column_names_centered = TRUE,
  border = TRUE,
  cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
    p <- sn_pb_padj_mat[i, j]
    if (is.finite(p) && p < 0.1) {
      grid::grid.text("*", x, y,
                      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"))
    }
  }
)
```

## hdWGCNA module MG-M3 echoes the interaction

An unsupervised co-expression module carries the same echo. Of four hdWGCNA modules, only MG-M3 has a negative interaction response (logFC -2.91), collapsing under the amyloid-conditioned tau effect (logFC -3.80, FDR 6.7e-4). The DAM module MG-M2 instead activates under every NLGF contrast (logFC 5.0-6.2), the expected amyloid-driven DAM programme.

```{r hdwgcna_module_logfc_heatmap, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
# hdWGCNA per-module eigengene DE: module logFC across the five canonical
# contrasts, FDR star/dot annotation, MG-M3 highlighted. Replicates the
# `hdwgcna-module-de-heatmap` chunk of rmd/03_hdwgcna.Rmd but loads the
# long-form result TSV instead of the hdWGCNA cache. MG-M3 carries the
# tau_in_nlgf / interaction transcriptional response (the unsupervised
# co-expression echo of the otherwise-null whole-microglia interaction).
hdw_lfc_de <- rt("hdwgcna_module_de.tsv")

# Contrast display order (mechanistic narrative) + readable axis labels.
hdw_lfc_ct_order <- c("tau_alone", "nlgf_in_maptki", "nlgf_in_p301s",
                      "tau_in_nlgf", "interaction")
hdw_lfc_ct_labels <- c(
  tau_alone      = "tau alone\n(P301S - MAPTKI)",
  nlgf_in_maptki = "NLGF in MAPTKI\n(NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI)",
  nlgf_in_p301s  = "NLGF in P301S\n(NLGF_P301S - P301S)",
  tau_in_nlgf    = "tau in NLGF\n(NLGF_P301S - NLGF_MAPTKI)",
  interaction    = "interaction\n(tau x amyloid)"
)

# Wide logFC and adj.P matrices: modules (rows) x contrasts (cols).
hdw_lfc_mods <- sort(unique(hdw_lfc_de$module),
                     method = "radix")
hdw_lfc_mods <- hdw_lfc_mods[
  order(suppressWarnings(as.integer(sub("^MG-M", "", hdw_lfc_mods))))
]
hdw_lfc_fc <- matrix(NA_real_, length(hdw_lfc_mods), length(hdw_lfc_ct_order),
                     dimnames = list(hdw_lfc_mods, hdw_lfc_ct_order))
hdw_lfc_pa <- hdw_lfc_fc
for (i in seq_len(nrow(hdw_lfc_de))) {
  hdw_lfc_fc[hdw_lfc_de$module[i], hdw_lfc_de$contrast[i]] <- hdw_lfc_de$logFC[i]
  hdw_lfc_pa[hdw_lfc_de$module[i], hdw_lfc_de$contrast[i]] <- hdw_lfc_de$adj.P.Val[i]
}
colnames(hdw_lfc_fc) <- hdw_lfc_ct_labels[colnames(hdw_lfc_fc)]
colnames(hdw_lfc_pa) <- colnames(hdw_lfc_fc)

# Diverging colour scale centred on 0, symmetric to the largest |logFC|.
hdw_lfc_rng <- max(abs(hdw_lfc_fc[is.finite(hdw_lfc_fc)]), na.rm = TRUE)
hdw_lfc_col <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-hdw_lfc_rng, 0, hdw_lfc_rng),
                                    c("#3a4cc0", "white", "#b40426"))

# FDR annotation: '*' = FDR<0.05, '.' = FDR<0.1; print the logFC otherwise.
hdw_lfc_cell <- function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
  pa <- hdw_lfc_pa[i, j]
  fc <- hdw_lfc_fc[i, j]
  if (is.na(fc)) return(invisible(NULL))
  star <- if (!is.na(pa) && pa < 0.05) " *" else if (!is.na(pa) && pa < 0.1) " ." else ""
  grid::grid.text(sprintf("%.1f%s", fc, star), x, y,
                  gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                  fontface = if (nzchar(star)) "bold" else "plain"))
}

# Highlight MG-M3 (the interaction/tau_in_nlgf carrier) with a red, bold
# row label; leave the others black.
hdw_lfc_lab_col <- ifelse(rownames(hdw_lfc_fc) == "MG-M3", "#b40426", "black")
hdw_lfc_lab_face <- ifelse(rownames(hdw_lfc_fc) == "MG-M3", "bold", "plain")

ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(
  hdw_lfc_fc,
  name            = "module\nlogFC",
  col             = hdw_lfc_col,
  na_col          = "grey92",
  cluster_rows    = FALSE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  column_order    = seq_len(ncol(hdw_lfc_fc)),
  cell_fun        = hdw_lfc_cell,
  row_names_side  = "left",
  row_names_gp    = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, col = hdw_lfc_lab_col,
                               fontface = hdw_lfc_lab_face),
  column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8),
  column_names_rot = 35,
  column_title    = paste0("hdWGCNA module-eigengene logFC across contrasts",
                           "  ('*' FDR<0.05, '.' FDR<0.1; MG-M3 in red)"),
  column_title_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11),
  heatmap_legend_param = list(legend_height = grid::unit(3, "cm")),
  border          = TRUE,
  rect_gp         = grid::gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1)
)
```

## Neighbourhood abundance shift, tau-asymmetric

At the abundance level, miloR neighbourhood DA shows NLGF expanding the DAM island and depleting the homeostatic island on both backgrounds. But detectability is tau-asymmetric: 132/1,023 neighbourhoods clear SpatialFDR < 0.1 on the P301S background versus *none* on MAPTKI, despite near-identical raw DAM-fraction shifts (0.19 to 0.37 vs 0.18 to 0.40).

```{r milo_da_umap_nlgf, fig.width=10, fig.height=5.2}
# miloR microglia differential-abundance, projected onto the substate UMAP.
# Each point is one neighbourhood placed at its index-cell UMAP coordinate;
# colour = DA log2FC oriented so POSITIVE means the neighbourhood is enriched
# in the NLGF (amyloid) arm of the pair (the milo design uses the NLGF level as
# the intercept, so its enrichment is the negative testNhoods coefficient; we
# flip the sign here for intuitive reading). Significant neighbourhoods
# (SpatialFDR < 0.1) are drawn at full opacity and outlined; non-significant
# ones are faded. Source: rmd/10_divergence.Rmd milo chunk; pre-extracted to
# summary_milo_da_umap.rds (index-cell UMAP + logFC/SpatialFDR + state + size).
milo_da_umap_tbl <- rd("summary_milo_da_umap.rds")
milo_da_umap_tbl$contrast <- factor(
  milo_da_umap_tbl$contrast,
  levels = c("NLGF in MAPTKI background", "NLGF in P301S background")
)
milo_da_umap_tbl$state <- factor(
  milo_da_umap_tbl$state,
  levels = c("homeostatic", "DAM", "IFN", "proliferative")
)
milo_da_umap_tbl$sig <- milo_da_umap_tbl$SpatialFDR < 0.1

# DAM-cluster hull centroid (for the substate callout) from the index cells.
milo_da_umap_dam <- milo_da_umap_tbl |>
  dplyr::filter(contrast == "NLGF in P301S background", state == "DAM") |>
  dplyr::summarise(umap1 = median(umap1), umap2 = median(umap2))

milo_da_umap_lim <- function(v) c(min(v) - 0.4, max(v) + 0.4)

milo_da_umap_p <- ggplot(
  milo_da_umap_tbl,
  aes(umap1, umap2)
) +
  geom_point(
    data = ~ dplyr::filter(.x, !sig),
    aes(size = nhood_size, fill = logFC_nlgf),
    shape = 21, colour = NA, alpha = 0.30
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ~ dplyr::filter(.x, sig),
    aes(size = nhood_size, fill = logFC_nlgf),
    shape = 21, colour = "grey20", stroke = 0.25, alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = milo_da_umap_dam, aes(label = "DAM"),
    size = 3.4, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey15",
    nudge_y = 1.1, segment.colour = "grey50", min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#3a4cc0", mid = "white", high = "#b40426", midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-5, 5), oob = scales::squish,
    name = expression(log[2]~FC~"(+ = NLGF-enriched)")
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.6, 4.2), name = "neighbourhood size") +
  facet_wrap(~ contrast) +
  coord_equal(
    xlim = milo_da_umap_lim(milo_da_umap_tbl$umap1),
    ylim = milo_da_umap_lim(milo_da_umap_tbl$umap2)
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Microglia neighbourhood differential abundance (miloR)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "NLGF reshapes substate composition on both tau backgrounds; ",
      "the DAM island is enriched in the NLGF arm"
    ),
    x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2",
    caption = paste0(
      "1,023 neighbourhoods; outlined points SpatialFDR < 0.1 ",
      "(132 significant for NLGF-in-P301S, 0 for NLGF-in-MAPTKI at this threshold). ",
      "Sign flipped so positive log2FC = enriched in NLGF."
    )
  )
milo_da_umap_p
```

# The mechanism layer

Resolved to kinases, intercellular signalling and pathway families, the divergence becomes concrete -- and one result dominates.

## Phosphosite stoichiometric correction

A methodological prerequisite first. Subtracting the matched parent-protein abundance from each phosphosite isolates phosphorylation stoichiometry before limma is refit. Raw and corrected site-level log2FC correlate only moderately (Spearman rho 0.71/0.71 across the two NLGF contrasts, ~12.8k sites each), so a substantial fraction of apparent phospho-regulation is parent-protein-driven and is removed here. All kinase inference downstream runs on the corrected estimates.

```{r prot_phospho_correction_scatter, fig.width=9, fig.height=5}
# Phospho-protein stoichiometric correction (rmd/06b_phospho_corr.Rmd):
# raw phosphosite log2FC mixes parent-protein abundance changes with
# site-level phosphorylation stoichiometry. Subtracting the matched
# parent-protein log2-intensity per sample and refitting limma isolates
# the stoichiometric component. This scatter compares raw vs
# protein-corrected log2FC on the two NLGF contrasts (publication_mode
# = FALSE, so the MAPT-KI contrast is shown). Off-diagonal points are
# sites whose apparent regulation was partly or wholly driven by the
# parent protein. Minimal plotting frame pre-extracted to
# storage/cache/summary_prot_phospho_correction_scatter.rds.
prot_corr_d <- rd("summary_prot_phospho_correction_scatter.rds")
prot_corr_df <- prot_corr_d$df
prot_corr_df$contrast_label <- factor(
  prot_corr_df$contrast_label,
  levels = unname(prot_corr_d$contrasts))

# Per-facet Spearman rho + n for the strip subtitle.
prot_corr_lab <- data.frame(
  contrast      = names(prot_corr_d$rho),
  contrast_label = unname(prot_corr_d$contrasts[names(prot_corr_d$rho)]),
  rho           = unname(prot_corr_d$rho),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
prot_corr_lab$n <- as.integer(table(prot_corr_df$contrast)[prot_corr_lab$contrast])
prot_corr_lab$contrast_label <- factor(prot_corr_lab$contrast_label,
                                       levels = unname(prot_corr_d$contrasts))
prot_corr_lab$txt <- sprintf("Spearman rho = %.3f\nn = %d sites",
                             prot_corr_lab$rho, prot_corr_lab$n)

# Axis range for placing the annotation in the lower-right of each panel.
prot_corr_xr <- range(prot_corr_df$raw, na.rm = TRUE)
prot_corr_yr <- range(prot_corr_df$corr, na.rm = TRUE)

prot_corr_p <- ggplot(prot_corr_df, aes(raw, corr)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "#b40426",
              linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.18, size = 0.5, colour = "grey25") +
  geom_text(data = prot_corr_lab,
            aes(x = prot_corr_xr[2], y = prot_corr_yr[1], label = txt),
            hjust = 1, vjust = 0, size = 3, colour = "#b40426",
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ contrast_label) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Phosphosite stoichiometric correction: raw vs parent-protein-corrected log2FC",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      paste0("Corrected = phospho minus matched parent-protein log2-intensity, refit by limma ",
             "(%s sites had a matched parent; %s retained after prevalence filter).",
             "\nDashed red = identity (no protein-driven component)."),
      format(prot_corr_d$n_sites_with_parent, big.mark = ","),
      format(prot_corr_d$n_sites_tested, big.mark = ",")),
    x = expression(raw~phospho~log[2]~fold-change),
    y = expression(corrected~(phospho-protein)~log[2]~fold-change)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
prot_corr_p
```

## Gsk3b activation at the interaction (headline)

The headline. decoupleR kinase-activity inference over the OmniPath kinase-substrate network puts **Gsk3b at +4.32 (ULM) at the interaction, FDR 0.00152** (consensus FDR 1.8e-4) -- the strongest single-mechanism signal in the project. The pattern is a tau-dependent sign-flip: Gsk3b is non-significantly negative when amyloid lands on wild-type tau (nlgf_in_maptki -1.66, ns) but strongly positive when it lands on a tau-mutant background (nlgf_in_p301s +3.79, FDR 0.0077). Gsk3b activation is therefore specifically a tau x amyloid synergy. (Bulk hippocampal phospho, not microglia-sorted: read as kinase activity anywhere in the tissue at this contrast.)

```{r prot_gsk3b_kinase_interaction, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
# Headline T-Synergy result: Gsk3b activation at the tau x amyloid
# interaction, the strongest single-mechanism signal in the project.
# Kinase activity = decoupleR ULM + consensus over the OmniPath
# kinase-substrate network (human -> mouse via nichenetr), run on the
# protein-corrected phospho DE cache (rmd/14_kinase_inference.Rmd).
# Reproduces the section-15.1 heatmap: the union of kinases reaching
# FDR<0.10 (ulm) in ANY contrast, cell colour = signed consensus
# score across all five contrasts (non-significant cells shown too so
# the Gsk3b sign-flip is visible: negative on the two backgrounds
# where amyloid does not activate it, strongly positive on the three
# where it does). '*' = FDR<0.05, '.' = FDR<0.10 on ulm.
prot_gsk3b_pc <- rt("kinase_activity_per_contrast.tsv")

# Verify the headline number is carried into the figure title.
prot_gsk3b_hit <- prot_gsk3b_pc[
  prot_gsk3b_pc$contrast == "interaction" & prot_gsk3b_pc$source == "Gsk3b", ]
prot_gsk3b_padj <- signif(prot_gsk3b_hit$padj_ulm[1], 3)
prot_gsk3b_score <- round(prot_gsk3b_hit$score_ulm[1], 2)

prot_gsk3b_title <- sprintf(
  paste0("Kinase activity x contrast (sig at FDR<0.10 ulm in any contrast)\n",
         "Gsk3b is the headline T-Synergy signal: +%.2f at the interaction, ",
         "FDR %s\n('*' = FDR<0.05, '.' = FDR<0.10; rows alphabetical; ",
         "colour = signed consensus score)"),
  prot_gsk3b_score, format(prot_gsk3b_padj, scientific = FALSE))

prot_gsk3b_hm <- plot_kinase_activity_heatmap(
  prot_gsk3b_pc,
  padj_cut         = 0.10,
  padj_strict      = 0.05,
  sig_statistic    = "ulm",
  colour_statistic = "consensus",
  title            = prot_gsk3b_title
)
prot_gsk3b_hm
```

## DAM-dominated ligand-receptor rewiring

The intercellular layer rewires where the transcriptome does not. Across the top-25 microglia-receiver ligand-receptor pairs prioritised for the interaction (MultiNicheNet), DAM is the receiver in 18/25 and sender in 10/25, and the ligands collapse to four macrophage-trophic/clearance molecules (Apoe, C1qb, Csf1, Icam1) -- including the Apoe->Trem2 clearance route. None of these LR genes overlaps an hdWGCNA hub, so the signalling signal sits off the module axis.

```{r ccc_interaction_lr_microglia, fig.width=9, fig.height=7.5}
# Top-25 microglia-receiver ligand-receptor pairs under the divergence
# INTERACTION contrast (MultiNicheNet prioritisation). This is the
# cell-cell-communication face of the project's "interaction signal is
# DAM-dominated" headline: where the whole-microglia transcriptional
# interaction is null, the intercellular LR layer surfaces a coherent,
# DAM-centred rewiring driven by a handful of macrophage-trophic /
# clearance ligands (Apoe, C1qb, Csf1, Icam1). Reproduces the
# `ccc-multinichenet-interaction-spotlight` table from rmd/11_ccc.Rmd as
# a ranked dot chart (TSV-backed; the 277M MNN cache is NOT loaded).
ccc_lr_tbl <- rt("ccc_multinichenet_interaction_top25_microglia.tsv")

# Compact substate labels for axis legibility (drop the "Microglia_"
# stem; the panel is microglia-receiver by construction).
ccc_lr_short <- function(x) sub("^Microglia_", "", x)
ccc_lr_tbl <- ccc_lr_tbl |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    sender_s   = ccc_lr_short(sender),
    receiver_s = ccc_lr_short(receiver),
    # DAM participation: the headline is that DAM dominates BOTH the
    # sender and receiver ends of the prioritised interaction LR space.
    dam_role = dplyr::case_when(
      sender == "Microglia_DAM" & receiver == "Microglia_DAM" ~ "DAM->DAM",
      sender == "Microglia_DAM"                               ~ "DAM sender",
      receiver == "Microglia_DAM"                             ~ "DAM receiver",
      TRUE                                                    ~ "Other"
    ),
    # Row label: ligand-receptor pair + the sender->receiver substate route.
    row_lab = sprintf("%s → %s  (%s→%s)",
                      ligand, receptor, sender_s, receiver_s)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(prioritization_score) |>
  dplyr::mutate(row_lab = factor(row_lab, levels = row_lab))

ccc_lr_dam_cols <- c(
  "DAM->DAM"     = "#B0344D",
  "DAM receiver" = "#E08754",
  "DAM sender"   = "#FDC086",
  "Other"        = "grey70"
)

ccc_lr_n_dam_recv <- sum(ccc_lr_tbl$receiver == "Microglia_DAM")
ccc_lr_n_dam_send <- sum(ccc_lr_tbl$sender   == "Microglia_DAM")
ccc_lr_ligands    <- paste(sort(unique(ccc_lr_tbl$ligand)), collapse = ", ")

ccc_lr_p <- ggplot(
  ccc_lr_tbl,
  aes(x = prioritization_score, y = row_lab, colour = dam_role)
) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = min(ccc_lr_tbl$prioritization_score) - 0.004,
                   xend = prioritization_score,
                   yend = row_lab),
               colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = ccc_lr_dam_cols, name = "DAM role",
                      breaks = names(ccc_lr_dam_cols)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)) +
  labs(
    title = "CCC interaction contrast: top-25 microglia-receiver LR pairs",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "MultiNicheNet prioritisation; (NLGF_P301S - P301S) - (NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI). ",
      "DAM is receiver in ", ccc_lr_n_dam_recv, "/25, sender in ",
      ccc_lr_n_dam_send, "/25"),
    x = "Prioritisation score", y = NULL,
    caption = paste0(
      "Ligands collapse to ", ccc_lr_ligands,
      "; clearance route Apoe→Trem2 present. ",
      "No ligand/receptor overlaps an hdWGCNA hub (per-row flags all FALSE)."
    )
  )
ccc_lr_p
```

## Axis-2 clearance: TREM2/APP over complement

Adjudicating the synaptic-suppression axis across three CCC tools (6,459 universe-filtered LR cells), TREM2-mediated clearance (Apoe_Trem2 rank 9), APP-fragment uptake (App_Cd74 rank 12) and glial adhesion rank shallow, whereas classical complement ranks deep (first C1qb_Lrp1 at rank 775; no C3 or Mertk-Pros1 in the top 800). The clearance signature, not textbook complement pruning, is what the data surface. The residual TAM-kinase Pros1_Mertk route survives separately at IFN-microglia nodes.

```{r ccc_axis2_lr_adjudication, fig.width=9.5, fig.height=6.5}
# Axis-2 (synaptic-suppression) cross-tool LR adjudication: which intercellular
# clearance mechanism the triangulated CCC layer surfaces, versus the textbook
# classical-complement synaptic-pruning pairs. Source: rmd/15_ccc_mechanism.Rmd
# section 16.2/16.3 (synaptic-suppression axis). Reproduces the axis-restricted
# ranking from ccc_lr_axis_restricted.tsv; classifies the canonical
# synaptic-clearance LR biochemistries into mechanism families and plots the
# best (shallowest) axis-2 rank reached by each, so the reader sees that
# TREM2-mediated clearance + APP-fragment uptake + glial adhesion rank near the
# top of the axis while classical complement / engulfment regulators rank deep.
ccc_ax2_axis <- rt("ccc_lr_axis_restricted.tsv")
ccc_ax2_axis <- ccc_ax2_axis[ccc_ax2_axis$axis == "synaptic_suppression", , drop = FALSE]

# Mechanism families keyed on the ligand_receptor biochemistry (cell-type pair
# is retained for the best-ranked node label). British-English family labels.
ccc_ax2_family <- function(lr) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    lr == "Apoe_Trem2"   ~ "TREM2-mediated clearance",
    lr == "App_Cd74"     ~ "APP-fragment uptake",
    lr == "Vcan_Cd44"    ~ "Glial adhesion modulation",
    lr == "Ncam1_Ptprz1" ~ "Glial adhesion modulation",
    lr == "Cd200_Cd200r1" ~ "Neuronal 'don't-activate-me'",
    grepl("^C1q",  lr)   ~ "Classical complement",
    grepl("^C3(_|ar1)", lr) ~ "Classical complement",
    grepl("^C4",   lr)   ~ "Classical complement",
    lr == "Cd47_Sirpa"   ~ "Engulfment regulator ('don't-eat-me')",
    lr == "Pros1_Mertk"  ~ "TAM-kinase phagocytosis",
    grepl("Mertk$|^Gas6_(Mertk|Tyro3|Axl)$", lr) ~ "TAM-kinase phagocytosis",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}
ccc_ax2_axis$family <- ccc_ax2_family(ccc_ax2_axis$lr_interaction)
ccc_ax2_keep <- ccc_ax2_axis[!is.na(ccc_ax2_axis$family), , drop = FALSE]

# Best (shallowest) axis-2 rank per LR biochemistry, with the cell-type node
# that achieves it (for the annotation label).
ccc_ax2_best <- ccc_ax2_keep |>
  dplyr::group_by(lr_interaction, family) |>
  dplyr::slice_min(leader_rank, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    node  = paste0(sender, " → ", receiver),
    label = paste0(lr_interaction, "  (", node, ")")
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(leader_rank)

# Pros1_Mertk is absent from the axis-2 universe-restricted ranking but DOES
# survive in the cross-tool cross-contrast leader board at Microglia_IFN-sender
# microglia nodes (3/5 contrasts, consistent +). Surface it as a residual route
# flagged separately rather than dropped, mirroring the section verdict prose.
ccc_ax2_lb <- rt("ccc_lr_cross_tool_leaderboard.tsv")
ccc_ax2_pm <- ccc_ax2_lb[ccc_ax2_lb$lr_interaction == "Pros1_Mertk", , drop = FALSE]
ccc_ax2_pm <- ccc_ax2_pm[order(-ccc_ax2_pm$n_contrasts_leader,
                               -ccc_ax2_pm$max_mean_norm_score), , drop = FALSE][1, ]

# Order families top->bottom by their shallowest rank so the clearance families
# sit at the top (small rank = strong axis-2 signal) and complement at the base.
ccc_ax2_fam_order <- ccc_ax2_best |>
  dplyr::group_by(family) |>
  dplyr::summarise(best = min(leader_rank), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::arrange(best) |>
  dplyr::pull(family)
ccc_ax2_best$family <- factor(ccc_ax2_best$family, levels = rev(ccc_ax2_fam_order))

# Two-tone palette: warm = clearance / adhesion (surfaced by the LR layer);
# cool grey-blue = classical complement / engulfment regulators (rank deep).
ccc_ax2_surfaced <- c("TREM2-mediated clearance", "APP-fragment uptake",
                      "Glial adhesion modulation", "Neuronal 'don't-activate-me'")
ccc_ax2_best$surfaced <- ifelse(as.character(ccc_ax2_best$family) %in% ccc_ax2_surfaced,
                                "Surfaced by LR layer", "Ranks deep (classical pruning)")

ccc_ax2_n2 <- nrow(ccc_ax2_axis)   # total axis-2 universe-filtered LR cells

ccc_ax2_p <- ggplot(ccc_ax2_best,
                    aes(x = leader_rank, y = stats::reorder(label, -leader_rank))) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = 0.7, yend = label, colour = surfaced),
               linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = surfaced, size = mean_activity_in_axis_contrasts)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 12.5, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 100, linetype = "dotted",
             colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 775),
                labels = c("1", "10", "100", "775")) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Surfaced by LR layer" = "#B0344D",
               "Ranks deep (classical pruning)" = "#7FA8C1"),
    name = NULL) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1.8, 5.5),
                        name = "cross-tool\nnorm. score") +
  facet_grid(family ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", colour = NA),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right",
        legend.box = "vertical",
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 7.5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Axis-2 synaptic suppression: which clearance mechanism the cross-tool LR layer surfaces",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Best axis-2 rank per ligand-receptor biochemistry (CellChat + MultiNicheNet + LIANA+; ",
      ccc_ax2_n2, " universe-filtered LR cells)"),
    x = "best axis-2 rank reached (log scale; lower = stronger)",
    y = NULL,
    caption = paste0(
      "Dashed line = top-12 cut; dotted = top-100 cut. TREM2 / APP-fragment / glial-adhesion edges rank shallow (Apoe_Trem2 rank 9, App_Cd74 rank 12);\n",
      "classical complement ranks deep (first C1qb_Lrp1 rank 775; Cd47_Sirpa rank 279; no C3 / Mertk-Pros1 in top 800). Residual TAM-kinase Pros1_Mertk\n",
      "is absent from the axis-2 universe ranking but survives in the cross-tool leader board at ", ccc_ax2_pm$sender, " → ", ccc_ax2_pm$receiver,
      " (", ccc_ax2_pm$n_contrasts_leader, "/5 contrasts, consistent +)."
    )
  )
ccc_ax2_p
```

## Unified pathway leader board: three axes

Pooling six gene-set collections under one fixed leader rule, the board refuses to name a single winner: three axes coexist -- tau x amyloid metabolic/translational non-additivity (mixed sign, led by MG-M3 at 23.4 with the OXPHOS/ETC family and DAM_up at 16.4), amyloid-driven activation (positive; MG-M2, adaptive immunity), and NLGF synaptic suppression (negative; presynaptic GO CC terms). The mixed-sign interaction family reaches breadth 4/4 substates.

```{r pathway_survey_leaderboard, fig.width=10, fig.height=8.5}
# Unified cross-modality / cross-collection pathway leader board (section 12
# verdict figure). One fixed leader rule was applied verbatim to every
# (pathway, contrast) cell of the six collection rankings; leader_score =
# 5*n_contrasts_consistent_sign_ge2 + n_contrasts_sig_ge3 + max_consistent_sign/5.
# Read the pre-built leader board TSV directly (15 KB) - no recomputation.
psl_lb <- rt("pathway_survey_unified_leaderboard.tsv")

# Strip GO prefixes and lower-case GO names for display; custom / module
# names (MG-M3, DAM_up, ...) lack a recognised prefix and pass through.
psl_pretty <- function(p) {
  is_go <- grepl("^(GOBP|GOMF|GOCC)_", p)
  p[is_go] <- gsub("_", " ", tolower(sub("^(GOBP|GOMF|GOCC)_", "", p[is_go])))
  p
}

# Top N leader pathways by composite leader_score (the section's View 1).
psl_top <- psl_lb |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(leader_score), collection, pathway) |>
  head(24) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    label = psl_pretty(pathway),
    # disambiguate the few identical pretty labels across GO collections
    label = ifelse(duplicated(label) | duplicated(label, fromLast = TRUE),
                   paste0(label, " (", toupper(sub("go", "GO ", collection)), ")"),
                   label),
    axis = dplyr::case_when(
      dominant_sign == "+"     ~ "Amyloid activation (+)",
      dominant_sign == "-"     ~ "Synaptic suppression (−)",
      dominant_sign == "mixed" ~ "Metabolic / translational (mixed sign)"
    ),
    axis = factor(axis, levels = c(
      "Amyloid activation (+)",
      "Synaptic suppression (−)",
      "Metabolic / translational (mixed sign)")),
    breadth_lab = ifelse(max_substate_breadth == 4L,
                         "breadth 4/4 substates", "")
  )
# Order bars by score (largest at top) within the plot.
psl_top$label <- factor(psl_top$label,
                        levels = rev(psl_top$label[order(psl_top$leader_score)]))

psl_cols <- c(
  "Amyloid activation (+)"                 = "#b40426",
  "Synaptic suppression (−)"          = "#3a4cc0",
  "Metabolic / translational (mixed sign)" = "#7b3294")

psl_p <- ggplot(psl_top,
                aes(x = leader_score, y = label, colour = axis)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = leader_score, yend = label),
               linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.55) +
  geom_point(aes(size = max_consistent_sign)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = contrasts_summary), hjust = 0, nudge_x = 0.45,
            size = 2.5, colour = "grey25") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = psl_cols, name = "Leader axis") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1.6, 4.2),
                        name = "Max sign-consistent\nsig modalities") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.42))) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.title.position = "plot") +
  labs(
    title = "Unified cross-modality pathway leader board surfaces three axes, not one winner",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Top 24 of ", nrow(psl_lb),
      " leader pathways pooled across GO BP/MF/CC + curated sets + hdWGCNA modules; ",
      "one fixed leader rule, five contrasts"),
    x = "leader_score (cross-contrast × cross-modality composite)",
    y = NULL,
    caption = paste0(
      "Text = per-contrast leader breakdown 'contrast:n_modalities/sign'. ",
      "MG-M3 (23.4) leads via mixed-sign non-additivity at the tau×amyloid interaction; ",
      "the OXPHOS/ETC family + DAM_up co-cluster at 16.4 with the same interaction:3/mixed signature; ",
      "amyloid-activation (MG-M2, adaptive immunity) is + and synaptic terms are −."
    ))

psl_p
```

# Integrated synthesis

The claims ledger adjudicates the competing models. On all three axes the data favour a divergent model over a simple additive one, and the cross-axis themes rank consistently.

## Tau attenuates NF-kB across all substates

The axis-1 mechanism, resolved. Tau on the amyloid background suppresses amyloid-driven NF-kB output at the interaction in all four substates: the union NF-kB-target GSEA reverses from positive at both NLGF arms (NES ~+1.0 to +1.2) to negative at the interaction (DAM NES -1.25, padj 0.027), mirrored by the CollecTRI NF-kB-complex TF score (DAM -6.5, p 7e-11), DAM leading. The substate-composition ANOVA (tau:nlgf p 0.33, ns) rules out a Simpson's-paradox abundance artefact -- the attenuation is transcriptional and global.

```{r per_state_nfkb_attenuation, fig.width=9.5, fig.height=8}
# Per-state NF-kB target attenuation at the tau x amyloid interaction (section 18).
# Two panels from the pre-extracted minimal cache (TF complex consensus + GSEA
# union NES, both layers, all 5 contrasts x 4 substates):
#  (A) GSEA-layer union-NF-kB-target NES across all five contrasts per substate
#      -> the sign reversal: positive (NF-kB-target activation) at the two NLGF
#         arms, negative (suppression) at the interaction and tau_in_nlgf.
#  (B) interaction-contrast carrier evidence: independent TF-complex consensus
#      score and GSEA union NES, both negative in 4/4 substates, ordered
#      DAM > homeostatic > IFN > proliferative (DAM lead carrier).
psnfkb_d <- rd("summary_per_state_nfkb_attenuation.rds")
psnfkb_long <- psnfkb_d$long
psnfkb_states <- c("DAM", "homeostatic", "IFN", "proliferative")  # magnitude order

psnfkb_contrast_levels <- c("nlgf_in_maptki", "nlgf_in_p301s",
                            "interaction", "tau_in_nlgf", "tau_alone")
psnfkb_contrast_labels <- c(
  nlgf_in_maptki = "NLGF in\nMAPTKI",
  nlgf_in_p301s  = "NLGF in\nP301S",
  interaction    = "interaction",
  tau_in_nlgf    = "tau in\nNLGF",
  tau_alone      = "tau\nalone"
)

# --- Panel A: GSEA union NES across contrasts (sign-reversal heatmap-style tiles)
psnfkb_a_df <- psnfkb_long
psnfkb_a_df$state <- factor(psnfkb_a_df$state, levels = rev(psnfkb_states))
psnfkb_a_df$contrast <- factor(psnfkb_a_df$contrast,
                               levels = psnfkb_contrast_levels)
psnfkb_a_df$lab <- sprintf("%.2f%s", psnfkb_a_df$gsea_union_NES,
                           ifelse(!is.na(psnfkb_a_df$gsea_union_padj) &
                                    psnfkb_a_df$gsea_union_padj < 0.10, "*", ""))

psnfkb_a <- ggplot(psnfkb_a_df,
                   aes(contrast, state, fill = gsea_union_NES)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = lab), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#3b4cc0", mid = "white", high = "#b40426", midpoint = 0,
    name = "GSEA NES\n(NF-kB targets)", limits = c(-1.4, 1.4)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = psnfkb_contrast_labels) +
  labs(
    title = "A  NF-kB-target GSEA sign-reversal across contrasts",
    subtitle = "Union CollecTRI NF-kB target set; + = activation, - = suppression",
    x = NULL, y = "microglia substate") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.height = grid::unit(0.9, "lines"))

# --- Panel B: interaction-contrast carrier evidence, two independent layers
psnfkb_int <- psnfkb_long[psnfkb_long$contrast == "interaction", , drop = FALSE]
psnfkb_b_df <- rbind(
  data.frame(state = psnfkb_int$state, layer = "TF complex activity\n(consensus score)",
             value = psnfkb_int$tf_complex_score,
             sig = !is.na(psnfkb_int$tf_complex_p) & psnfkb_int$tf_complex_p < 0.10,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  data.frame(state = psnfkb_int$state, layer = "Target-set GSEA\n(union NES x4, scaled)",
             value = psnfkb_int$gsea_union_NES * 4,  # scale NES onto score axis for shared panel
             sig = !is.na(psnfkb_int$gsea_union_padj) & psnfkb_int$gsea_union_padj < 0.10,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
)
psnfkb_b_df$state <- factor(psnfkb_b_df$state, levels = rev(psnfkb_states))
psnfkb_b_df$layer <- factor(psnfkb_b_df$layer,
                            levels = c("TF complex activity\n(consensus score)",
                                       "Target-set GSEA\n(union NES x4, scaled)"))
psnfkb_b_df$siglab <- ifelse(psnfkb_b_df$sig, "FDR<0.10", "")

psnfkb_b <- ggplot(psnfkb_b_df, aes(value, state, fill = layer)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.68) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4, colour = "grey40") +
  geom_text(aes(label = siglab),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
            hjust = 1.05, size = 2.6, colour = "grey20") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TF complex activity\n(consensus score)" = "#5b8fb9",
                               "Target-set GSEA\n(union NES x4, scaled)" = "#d98994"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "B  Both layers carry suppression at the interaction",
    subtitle = "DAM lead carrier; 4/4 substates negative on both layers",
    x = "signed effect at interaction (negative = NF-kB suppression)",
    y = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.size = grid::unit(0.8, "lines"))

psnfkb_anova_f <- psnfkb_d$anova_taunlgf[["F value"]]
psnfkb_anova_p <- psnfkb_d$anova_taunlgf[["Pr(>F)"]]

(psnfkb_a / psnfkb_b) +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title = "Tau attenuates amyloid-driven NF-kB across all four microglia substates",
    subtitle = "Per-state NEBULA (1% prevalence); interaction = (NLGF_P301S - P301S) - (NLGF_MAPTKI - MAPTKI)",
    caption = sprintf(
      paste0("Verdict: Global attenuation (4/4 substates). Section 11 substate-composition ",
             "ANOVA tau:nlgf F=%.2f, p=%.3f (n.s.) rules out a Simpson's-paradox abundance artefact."),
      psnfkb_anova_f, psnfkb_anova_p),
    theme = ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9.5),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0)))
```

## Three-axis biological-model contest verdicts

Head-to-head, each axis favours its divergent model: axis 1 -> tau attenuates amyloid-driven NF-kB (margin 18), axis 2 -> TREM2/APP-fragment clearance over classical complement (margin 12), axis 3 -> a distinct tau x amyloid synergy mechanism (margin 55, the largest), anchored by Gsk3b. All three resolve at the first arithmetic level with no tie-break invoked.

```{r biomodel_contest_verdicts, results='asis'}
# Three-axis contest verdict table (section 17.3.1): the favoured biological
# model and its net-support margin for each of the project's three mechanism
# axes, derived from the H3 adjudication of the 81-row claims ledger. This is
# the synthesis headline: amyloid_activation -> Hyp-1B (tau attenuates NF-kB,
# margin 18), synaptic_suppression -> Hyp-2B (TREM2/APP clearance, margin 12),
# interaction_metabolic -> Hyp-3B (Gsk3b/Myc synergy, margin 53; carries the
# project's strongest single-kinase result Gsk3b padj 0.00152).
bmcv_axis_labels <- c(
  amyloid_activation    = "Axis 1 - Amyloid activation",
  synaptic_suppression  = "Axis 2 - Synaptic suppression",
  interaction_metabolic = "Axis 3 - Tau x amyloid interaction")

bmcv <- rt("biological_model_contest_verdicts.tsv")

bmcv_tbl <- bmcv |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Axis = unname(bmcv_axis_labels[contest_id]),
    `Favoured model` = sprintf("%s - %s",
                               favoured_model,
                               ifelse(favoured_model == model_a_id,
                                      model_a_name, model_b_name)),
    `Defeated model` = sprintf("%s - %s",
                               ifelse(favoured_model == model_a_id,
                                      model_b_id, model_a_id),
                               ifelse(favoured_model == model_a_id,
                                      model_b_name, model_a_name)),
    `Winner net (sup-con)` = ifelse(
      favoured_model == model_a_id,
      sprintf("%d (%d-%d)", model_a_net_support, model_a_support_count,
              model_a_contradict_count),
      sprintf("%d (%d-%d)", model_b_net_support, model_b_support_count,
              model_b_contradict_count)),
    `Winner Strong rows` = ifelse(
      favoured_model == model_a_id,
      model_a_n_strong_supports, model_b_n_strong_supports),
    `Margin` = favoured_by_margin
  )

# Stable axis order (1, 2, 3) regardless of TSV row order.
bmcv_tbl <- bmcv_tbl[order(match(bmcv$contest_id,
                                 c("amyloid_activation", "synaptic_suppression",
                                   "interaction_metabolic"))), ]

knitr::kable(
  bmcv_tbl,
  row.names = FALSE,
  align = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "r"),
  caption = paste0(
    "Per-axis contest verdicts. Each of the project's three mechanism axes ",
    "pairs two competing biological models head-to-head; the favoured model ",
    "is the one with the higher net support (support_count - contradict_count) ",
    "across the 81-row H2 claims ledger. **Margin** is the favoured model's ",
    "net support minus the loser's. All three resolve at the first arithmetic ",
    "level (no tie-break invoked). Axis 1's Hyp-1B is anchored by the Rela ",
    "sign-reversal (now replicated in all four microglia substates, section ",
    "18); axis 3's Hyp-3B by Gsk3b activation at the interaction (padj ",
    "0.00152, the project's strongest single-kinase result)."))
```

## Cross-axis theme support ranking

Ranking the cross-axis themes by supporting ledger rows: T-Inflammation 31 (6 Strong) > T-Synergy 30 (9 Strong) > T-Compartment-suppression 17 > T-Tau-attenuates 12 > Hyp-0 (Cdk5) 7. Additive DAM-amplification (T-Inflammation) now edges past the Gsk3b/Myc synergy theme on raw supporting-row count, though T-Synergy retains the most Strong-grade support (9 versus 6); the Cdk5 integrator remains the most evenly cross-axis-distributed entity. This is the project's standing synthesis.

```{r crossaxis_theme_ranking, fig.width=8.5, fig.height=5}
# Cross-axis theme support ranking (section 17.3.2): the four cross-axis themes
# plus the Hyp-0 Cdk5 integrator, ranked by support count with the confidence-
# grade decomposition (Strong / Moderate / Suggestive) stacked. Surfaces the
# established ranking T-Synergy (28) > T-Inflammation (26) > T-Compartment-
# suppression (17) > T-Tau-attenuates (11) > Hyp-0 Cdk5 (7), each row's number
# of Strong-grade supports annotated. Tallies are taken verbatim from the H3
# adjudication TSV (the same single-pass groupby the synthesis chapter renders).
catr <- rt("biological_model_adjudication.tsv")

# Themes + integrator only (the cross-axis entities); contest models are
# adjudicated head-to-head elsewhere (biomodel_contest_verdicts).
catr_ent <- catr[catr$entity_type %in% c("theme", "integrator"), , drop = FALSE]

catr_levels <- catr_ent$entity_id[order(catr_ent$support_count)]  # ascending -> bars bottom-to-top

catr_long <- data.frame(
  entity_id = rep(catr_ent$entity_id, 3),
  grade = rep(c("Strong", "Moderate", "Suggestive"),
              each = nrow(catr_ent)),
  n = c(catr_ent$n_strong_supports,
        catr_ent$n_moderate_supports,
        catr_ent$n_suggestive_supports),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
catr_long$entity_id <- factor(catr_long$entity_id, levels = catr_levels)
catr_long$grade <- factor(catr_long$grade,
                          levels = c("Suggestive", "Moderate", "Strong"))

# Total + Strong-count annotation per entity (placed at bar end).
catr_ann <- data.frame(
  entity_id = factor(catr_ent$entity_id, levels = catr_levels),
  total = catr_ent$support_count,
  n_strong = catr_ent$n_strong_supports,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
catr_ann$lab <- sprintf("%d  (%d Strong)", catr_ann$total, catr_ann$n_strong)

# Readable display names paired with the id.
catr_namemap <- setNames(catr_ent$entity_name, catr_ent$entity_id)
catr_axis_labeller <- function(ids) {
  sprintf("%s\n%s", ids, ifelse(nchar(catr_namemap[ids]) > 34,
                                paste0(substr(catr_namemap[ids], 1, 33), "..."),
                                catr_namemap[ids]))
}

catr_grade_cols <- c(Strong = "#1b6d4f", Moderate = "#6cae8f", Suggestive = "#c9e3d6")

ggplot(catr_long, aes(n, entity_id, fill = grade)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.68, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(data = catr_ann, aes(total, entity_id, label = lab),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = -0.08, size = 3.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = catr_grade_cols, name = "Confidence grade",
                    breaks = c("Strong", "Moderate", "Suggestive")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = catr_axis_labeller) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.22))) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-axis theme support ranking",
    subtitle = paste0("Supporting ledger rows per cross-axis entity, ",
                      "stacked by confidence grade (Strong / Moderate / Suggestive)"),
    x = "supporting claim-ledger rows",
    y = NULL,
    caption = paste0(
      "Counts from the H3 adjudication of the 81-row claims ledger. ",
      "T-Synergy is anchored at axis 3 (Gsk3b synergy); T-Tau-attenuates ",
      "lifted to 11 supports / 7 Strong by the Phase I per-state NF-kB test; ",
```
